pattern: Zero splits the array into independent runs, and within a run the product's sign only depends on how many negative numbers you've included -- so track the longest run ending here with an even and an odd count of negatives separately.
hints: A product is positive exactly when the number of negative factors in it is even, so what matters isn't the numbers themselves but a running parity count of negatives, reset at every zero.
Keep two running lengths as you scan: the longest subarray ending at the current position with a positive product, and the longest one ending there with a negative product.

Extending onto a positive number keeps both running lengths growing by one (or keeps the negative one at zero if it was already empty); extending onto a negative number swaps the two, adding one to whichever was tracking the opposite sign -- take the best positive-length seen at any point.

edge_inputs.description: a leading lone zero segment followed by a run whose longest positive-product window is not the whole run -- the zero itself contributes a segment of length zero, and within the remaining run [1,-2,-3,-4] the best positive-product window is [1,-2,-3] at length 3, not the full length-4 run
two negatives multiply to a positive product, so the whole pair counts
three negatives in a row means the full run's product is negative, so the best window drops one element
zeros scattered between mixed-sign runs of different best lengths
a uniform run at the maximum magnitude value, all positive
the whole array already has a positive product

stress.description: random mix of positive, negative, and zero values across a large array
